Method to define set of reference images to be tested.
Can be overridden to add additional output products to the test.
These must correspond to whatever is used in writing out the FITS files (eg. those in ClassDeconvMachine.py)
:returns: List of image identifiers to reference and output products

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures

\begin{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar

xt=arcsin(sin(x)*sin(y)+cos(x)*cos(y)*cos(z)) and
yt=arccos((sin(x)\sphinxhyphen{}sin(y)*sin(xt))/(cos(y)*cos(xt))) {]}

\end{description}

build soldir \sphinxhyphen{} gridmachine mapping
read ClusterCat
per clustercat dir, set DicoJonesDirToFacet
read model\_dict from self.FacetMachine.\_model\_dict; reload it
per\sphinxhyphen{}facet, get iDirJones, sumflux, sumI
if the iFacet sumflux is not zero, tabulate FacetsID and sumflux per jonesdir (tessel)
per\sphinxhyphen{}facet, read the grid, set the DicoImager and DIcoJonesDirToFacet
copy ClusterCat, NDirs to build new ones (in case of empty facets within tessels)
this class imports the beam from killMS.Data
read solve threshold from ImageSkyModel.
get apparent flux max per jones\sphinxhyphen{}dir.
if this appflux is less than overall max appflux * threshold, remove it.
reset mappings from original clustercat to kept one
reset NDirs etc
return True

self.raSol, self.decSol

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function reads the already\sphinxhyphen{}initialised default parset object.
It then redefines all options as part of an optparse call.
After this, it reads the command\sphinxhyphen{}line inputs for all options; defaults are kept when not provided via commandline.
Finally, it calls on killMS.Other.MyPickle to save the actual parset call for this run.
It returns the optparse object for this run.

\sphinxAtStartPar
defines a new MyOptParse object.
depends on ReadCFG, ClassPrint, Other.ModColor, Other.logo
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\textless{}module ‘killMS.Parset.MyOptParse’ from ‘/home/bonnassieux/Wirtinger/killMS/killMS/Parset/MyOptParse.py’\textgreater{}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}
